arts.ttl: Birmingham City vs Leyton Orient - TV details, live coverage and team news
arts.sum: Orient arrive having lost to Bolton last time out – but still in a good run of form with five wins from their last seven league matches and in sixth place in the table. Birmingham City play one of their two games in hand this evening when they welcome Leyton Orient to League One’s only unconquered outpost.

arts.ttl: Stansfield, Leonard, Anderson: Birmingham City injury latest before Leyton Orient
arts.sum: Blues travelled to the Select Car Leasing Stadium without talisman Jay Stansfield after their 20-goal top scorer sustained a leg injury against Bradford City last week. Scott Wright and Lyndon Dykes' names were added to Birmingham City's injury list during the 0-0 draw with Reading at the weekend.

arts.ttl: Birmingham City v Wrexham Vertu Trophy final disruption plan emerges
arts.sum: Darren Ferguson acknowledges that Peterborough United's priority is to preserve their League One status come the end of the season - but that won't stop Posh trying to deny the 'Hollywood Derby' final of Birmingham City v Wrexham in the Vertu Trophy final at Wembley in April. Birmingham City are heading to Wembley for the Vertu Trophy final in April - on Wednesday they'll discover if they're playing Peterborough or Wrexham

arts.ttl: Tuesday's League One predictions including Birmingham vs. Leyton Orient
arts.sum: Sports Mole provides score predictions and previews for all of today's League One fixtures, including Birmingham vs. Leyton Orient.

arts.ttl: Birmingham City tipped to destroy Wolves and on track for EFL record
arts.sum: The prediction from CasinoHawks' latest League One Supercomputer has Blues finishing on 108 points, which would see them topple Wolves and Reading’s long-standing records. Birmingham City have been tipped to shatter the League One points record Wolverhampton Wanderers set in 2014.

arts.ttl: How Birmingham City attendances would stack up in the Championship and the Premier League
arts.sum: Birmingham City have regularly enjoyed sell-out attendances at St Andrew's @ Knighthead Park this season Blues have been backed by sell-out crowds on a fortnightly basis this season with long suffering fans flocking to the revamped St Andrew’s @ Knighthead Park.

arts.ttl: Former Birmingham City defender 'extremely active' as he waits for new manager job
arts.sum: Former Birmingham City defender Jerry Gill has declared himself ready to return to the dugout, three months after leaving Bath City - and he considers himself a 'more rounded and improved' coach after spending his time out of the game wisely. Former Birmingham City defender Jerry Gill spent seven years at Bath City but is ready for another challenge in management
